mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer income and spending, driving corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns higher over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If monthly PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are negative in more than half of the observations over any rolling 2-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate by at least 0.3 percentage points in a single month signals a tightening labor market that accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by at least 0.3pp from prior month
failure_condition: If the trigger condition is met but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in at least 40% of such instances over the walk-forward period, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1% and year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 2%, real interest rates become deeply negative, encouraging borrowing and risk-taking that lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: If the joint condition is met but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered cases over a 3-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During an economic expansion phase, sustained quarterly growth in real GDP above 2% annualized signals robust aggregate demand that drives corporate profits and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
regime_hint: economic expansion phase (GDP growth positive and accelerating)
failure_condition: If during periods of expansion (GDPC1 q/q growth > 0) the forward 60-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than a third of observations, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 1.5 million units annualized, it indicates strong residential investment and consumer confidence, which translates into higher economic activity and positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If HOUST exceeds 1500 but the S&P 500 forward 20-day return is negative more than 40% of the time over any 2-year period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing and supply capacity, which boosts corporate output and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following month regardless of the economic cycle.
failure_condition: If monthly INDPRO growth is positive but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than half of observations over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in payroll employment (above 150k monthly change) and a decline in the unemployment rate signals strong labor demand that quickly tightens the labor market, boosting wage income and near-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 150 AND UNRATE month-over-month decrease
failure_condition: If the joint trigger condition is met but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 30% of cases over a 2-year walk-forward period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and raise borrowing costs, compressing valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > current level and rising over at least 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If during a confirmed tightening cycle the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is falsified.
